must_not_assert: that the operator was unauthorized or that a real-world compromise outside this capture is established
that EV-004 proves payload effects beyond the observed service binary execution as SYSTEM
that all created services were removed or that persistence remained active after the capture window

the_trap: The visible write-host test command and the Fax restore command can invite under-triage. However, a new service is created and started, and EV-004 confirms that its non-system binary executes from services.exe as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. The cleanup context limits claims about lasting persistence, but it does not undo the evidenced SYSTEM-level service execution.
rationale: A0 modifies an existing Windows service, EV-001 and EV-002 create and start a new service, and EV-003 creates and starts a second service. Most decisively, EV-004 records the configured non-system service binary executing under services.exe as NT AUTHORITY\SYSTEM. This confirms successful service-based privileged execution rather than a setup-only indicator. EV-005 is mitigating context because it issues a command to restore the Fax service, but it neither negates EV-004 nor proves removal of the two new services. Under this benchmark, malicious denotes decisive execution of an offensive security mechanism; it does not claim that the operator was unauthorized. Strong, malicious, high-critical; up-rank despite the test-like command and cleanup context.
